methods.name: Peer-Median

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Delian Alliance Industries is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $14M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. The peer set spans Seed through Series A stages within the defense and security sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $14M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $35M. Peer-median valuation benchmarks against five same-sector comparables—Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), NODA AI ($125M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed)—producing a median of $150M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

Blending these two methods yields a triangulated valuation range of $30M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $92M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: peer valuations cluster at Series A stage ($125M–$500M) while the single Seed comparable (Tenna Systems at $60M) anchors the lower bound. Key uncertainties include whether Delian Alliance's technology or market position justifies a premium or discount relative to the peer median, and whether the Series A comparables appropriately reflect risk-adjusted value for a Seed-stage company.
